market_summary: S&P 500 rose +0.6% for the week (April 21-25), closing at a fresh all-time high of 7,165.8 on Friday. Nasdaq gained +1.5% to 24,837 — also a record. Philadelphia Semiconductor Index (SOX) up 4%+ on Intel blowout earnings, 18-day win streak. US-Iran peace talks potentially resuming in Pakistan drove Friday's equity rally. Russell 2000 also hit all-time highs. Tech sector is #1 in momentum rankings (Alpha Z +1.85). Portfolio outperformed S&P 500 this week: +1.06% vs +0.60% = +0.46pp outperformance.
macro_themes: 1. IRAN/HORMUZ (DOMINANT): Ceasefire extended indefinitely by Trump on April 21, but Iran seized 2 more ships since ceasefire. Hormuz de facto closed. US-Iran peace talks may resume in Pakistan — if talks progress formally, 4-week GLD invalidation clock could start. Clock has NOT started as of April 27. 2. INFLATION: March 2026 CPI +3.3% YoY / +0.9% MoM — gasoline +21.2% MoM (largest since 1967). Stagflation risk with oil above $100/barrel. April CPI due May 12. 3. FED: FOMC April 28-29 expected to hold at 3.50-3.75% (Polymarket 99.9% probability). JPMorgan: no cuts in 2026, possible 25bps hike Q3 2027. U03A stage-down trigger will NOT fire this week. 4. AI CAPEX SUPERCYCLE: NVDA Vera Rubin in full production (3.3x speed improvement over Blackwell Ultra). Hyperscalers committing $600B+ in 2026 AI capex. 5. EARNINGS SEASON: AMZN Q1 April 29 (AWS mid-20% growth expected), MA Q1 April 30. 6. TARIFFS: 90-day pause on elevated tariff rates (ex-China 125%) continues. Trump threatening 50% additional tariff on China over alleged Iranian weapons supply.
portfolio_health: Grade: B+ (83/100). NAV $4,351.26. Week return +1.06% vs S&P 500 +0.60% = +0.46pp outperformance. YTD from April 4 seed ($4,191.00): +3.82%. All 6 positions with intact or strengthening theses. Cash at 8.44% is idle drag with May 3 hard deployment deadline 6 days away — plan in place. NVDA near all-time high ($208.27 vs ATH $212.19) — STRONGEST thesis. AMZN +23.39% unrealized with high-conviction earnings catalyst April 29. WHT structure excellent: 5 of 6 positions 0% WHT; MA WHT <$5/year annualized — immaterial. GLD WATCH flag maintained but buffer slightly improved to 3.1% above $420 trigger. Upcoming catalysts: FOMC April 28-29, AMZN earnings April 29, MA earnings April 30.

positions.rationale: May 3 hard cash deployment deadline is 6 days away. S&P 500 at all-time highs with tech momentum #1. Deploy $142.09 unconditionally at April 28 open. Any post-earnings residual cash also flows to CSPX by May 3. IE-domiciled accumulating structure remains most WHT-efficient deployment vehicle.

Q1 earnings April 29 at 5:30 PM ET. Consensus $1.63 EPS / $177.27B revenue; AWS growth expected mid-20% range. 42 Buy / 3 Hold; BofA $298, BMO $315, UBS $304, Goldman $290 price targets. Conditional ADD $75 post-earnings if AWS >25%, Q1 EBIT >$20B, Q2 guidance maintained or raised. No pre-earnings add warranted at +23.39% unrealized with overbought RSI.

WATCH flag maintained. Buffer above $420 trim trigger slightly improved to 3.1% (from 2.6%). Hormuz de facto closed — 4-week invalidation clock NOT started. Iran peace talks in Pakistan (Friday market catalyst) are a new threat to thesis; monitor closely. Stagflation backdrop (CPI +3.3%, oil >$100) provides support. Hard stop: trim 25% (~$108) into CSPX on any daily close below $420.

Q1 earnings April 30. Consensus $4.40 EPS (+18% YoY). 35 of 38 analysts Buy/Outperform, mean target $654.66 (+30% upside). Q1 covers Jan-March (pre-peak Iran oil shock) so cross-border volumes expected clean. Note ~$200M one-time restructuring charge — focus on adjusted EPS and operational metrics. Conditional ADD $100 if cross-border volume >14%, VAS growth >20%, guidance maintained or raised.

STRONGEST thesis. Near all-time high of $212.19. Vera Rubin AI servers in full production (3.3x speed improvement over Blackwell Ultra). 65.47% YoY revenue growth. Average analyst target ~$268 implying strong upside. Q1 FY2027 earnings May 20 or 27 (date conflict — verify via NVIDIA IR). No action warranted; RSI overbought. Hold through May earnings catalyst.

Capital preservation layer functioning as designed. FOMC April 28-29 expected to hold (99.9% probability) — stage-down trigger will NOT fire. YTM ~3.67% well above 3.0% trim threshold. Next stage-down review: June 2026 FOMC. Iran war sustains capital preservation rationale.

Deployment: (1) April 28 open — unconditional $142.09 into CSPX; (2) April 29 post-earnings — conditional $75 into AMZN if criteria met; (3) April 30 post-earnings — conditional $100 into MA if criteria met; (4) May 3 hard deadline — deploy all remaining into CSPX, retain $50 minimum buffer. Cash must be ≤$50 after May 3 under all circumstances. No extensions permitted.

new_opportunities.rationale: Broadcom is the highest-conviction new-position candidate. AI custom silicon leadership via Google TPU and Meta AI accelerator partnerships through 2029-2031. $73B+ AI backlog targeting $100B+ AI revenue by FY2027. VMware networking integration provides durable recurring revenue. Mizuho Buy rating, $480 target; rapidly expanding ASIC pipeline. Zero effective WHT (negligible dividend). Complementary to NVDA (custom XPUs vs standard GPUs). GATE: NAV must exceed $5,000 — current NAV $4,351.26 is $648.74 below threshold. Do NOT buy yet.

action_items: 1. CRITICAL — April 28 open: Deploy $142.09 into CSPX at market open unconditionally. May 3 hard deadline is 5 trading days away. No further deferral permitted.

2. CRITICAL — April 28-29 FOMC: Expected hold at 3.50-3.75%. Monitor Powell press conference language. If explicit dovish signal (rate cut window mentioned): trim U03A -$100 into CSPX immediately. If hold-unchanged or hawkish: no U03A action.

3. CRITICAL — April 29, 5:30 PM ET — AMZN Q1 earnings: ADD $75 post-earnings if ALL criteria met: (a) AWS growth >25% YoY, (b) Q1 EBIT >$20B, (c) Q2 guidance maintained or raised. HOLD if in-line. No EXIT unless EBIT below $16.5B floor with Q2 guide-down.

4. CRITICAL — April 30 — MA Q1 earnings: ADD $100 post-earnings if ALL criteria met: (a) cross-border volume >14%, (b) VAS growth >20%, (c) guidance maintained or raised. Focus on adjusted EPS (ignore ~$200M one-time restructuring charge optics). HOLD if in-line.

5. HARD DEADLINE — May 3: Deploy all remaining deployable cash into CSPX. If AMZN add not triggered: deploy extra $75 into CSPX. If MA add not triggered: deploy extra $100 into CSPX. Retain $50 minimum buffer. No extensions under any circumstances. Cash must be ≤$50 after May 3.

6. DAILY MONITOR — GLD $420 alert: GLD at $433.25 (3.1% buffer above trigger). If any daily close below $420: trim 25% of GLD (~$108) and redeploy into CSPX immediately. NEW: Monitor US-Iran peace talk progress — formal resumption and sustained ceasefire compliance could start 4-week GLD invalidation clock.

7. VERIFY — NVDA earnings date: Conflicting sources cite May 20 vs May 27 for Q1 FY2027 earnings. Verify via NVIDIA Investor Relations before the date. Prior thesis corrected to May 27 but latest research cites May 20 — confirm the correct date.
